steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank follows “Defined by a disorganized molecular structure,” — a participial phrase. Each choice offers a different noun to start the following clause. Identify: which noun appears as the subject immediately after the participial phrase?

steps.title: Apply the modifier rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase opening a sentence must be immediately followed by the noun it modifies. “Defined by a disorganized molecular structure” describes amorphous ice, so choice B (‘amorphous ice lacks the thermal energy to form the crystals found in ice Ih.’) is correct: “amorphous ice lacks the thermal energy to form the crystals found in ice Ih.” places amorphous ice as the subject right after the phrase.

The other choices start with different nouns, violating this rule.

That confirms B is correct.
